title: NOC 53121 – Actors, Comedians, and Circus Performers | GTR Immigration
description: Actors, Comedians, and Circus Performers under NOC 53121 are professional entertainers who perform in film, television, theatre, circuses, comedy shows, and other live or recorded productions. This occupation is classified under TEER 3, typically requiring short-term college training, specialized artistic training, or extensive practical experience. Canada’s entertainment industry has been expanding significantly, particularly in film production, streaming platforms, live entertainment, and performing arts, creating increasing opportunities for performers across the country.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation, and sportTEER3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of less than two years, or significant on-the-job trainingMajor Group53 – Occupations in art, culture, and sportSub-Major Group531 – Occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5312 – Occupations in creative and performing artNOC Code53121

duties: Professionals under NOC 53121 may perform some or all of the following duties:
Study and rehearse scripts, gestures, and expressions to perform roles
Portray characters in films, television programs, theatre productions, radio dramas, and commercials
Perform narration for documentaries or other productions
Sing or dance when required by specific roles
Perform comedy routines individually or as part of a comedy troupe
Improvise roles and develop original performances
Train students in acting techniques, voice control, movement, and dramatic interpretation
Prepare students for auditions and stage performances
Perform acrobatic, aerial, and physical acts in circus productions
Design and choreograph original performance routines
Practice specialized circus techniques such as juggling, aerial acts, or acrobatics
Contribute to creative direction under a ringmaster or director
employment_requirements: Demonstrated performance ability through auditions or prior roles is a key hiring factor
Circus performers may require training at a circus school or specialized academy
Acting programs are offered at universities, colleges, and private acting schools
Acting teachers generally require previous professional acting experience
Membership in professional unions or guilds may be required
salary_prospects: (Data may not be available for some provinces.)
Actors, Comedians, and Circus Performers typically earn:
$26,900 – $71,200 per year,depending on contracts, reputation, and experience.
High-profile performers may earn significantly more through major productions.
Canada continues to attract major film and television productions due to:
International film studios operating in Canada
This supports continued demand for skilled performers.
